verificationLabel: Round 12 team lists checked Wednesday 21 May 2026
summary: Round 12 is an Origin-hit five-game slate, with the Bulldogs carrying the clearest roster edge on paper and no charity bet locked yet while the market settles.

scoreboard.note: Herbie Farnworth dropping out hurts, but Canberra are also heavily carved up by Origin with Ethan Strange and Hudson Young missing.
Canterbury regain Jacob Kiraz while Melbourne lose Munster, Grant and Loiero to Origin, making this the clearest structural edge of the round.
Roger Tuivasa-Sheck's return gives the Warriors more strike and they still look the cleaner side against a struggling Dragons outfit.
Manly are patched up, but the Titans also lose key Origin names and the safer lean still sits with the home side.
Taumalolo returns for North Queensland while South Sydney are without Latrell Mitchell and Campbell Graham, which drags the game toward the Cowboys.

liveAlerts.text: Round 12 is shorter, so taking the cleaner Origin-depletion reads matters more than usual.
The Mark Hughes Foundation ledger stays at 2-2 and -$3.50, so no charity bet should be forced without a clear price edge.
Bulldogs versus Storm is the clearest roster-tilt matchup of the week.
Raiders versus Dolphins and Sea Eagles versus Titans both have enough personnel churn to punish overconfidence.
Roger Tuivasa-Sheck back for the Warriors and Kiraz back for the Bulldogs are the key reinforcements to respect.
No Round 12 charity bet is locked yet, which is disciplined on a messy Origin slate.
